sections.paragraph:
καθαρότης, ητος, ἡ (s. καθαρός; X., Mem. 2, 1, 22 al.; Caecilius the orator [I b.c.] p. 98, 7; 107, 1 EOfenloch [1907] of purity of speech; Epict. 4, 11, 5; POxy 67, 6; 904, 2; Ex 24:10 v.l.; Wsd 7:24; EpArist 234; TestNapht 3:1; τοῦ νοῦ κ. Did., Gen. 114, 10; Orig., C. Cels. 1, 26, 51 as quality of the pious) state or condition of being ritually cleansed, purity: τῆς σαρκός Hb 9:13 (s. also Iambl., Vita Pyth. 24, 106 καθαρότης τῆς ψυχῆς; Did., Gen. 151, 11). ἐν καθαρότη[τι καταστήσει] [will provide lodgings] in purity (=‘will practice purity while providing lodgings [for the young women]’) Hs 10, 3, 4 (POxy 404 recto, 118f).—DELG s.v. καθαρός. M-M. TW.

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5
  2. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

TestNapht TestNapht = Testament of Naphthali, s. Test12Patr—List 2

Did. Did. = Didymus Caecus (the Blind), commentator on OT books, IV a.d.—List 5

Orig Orig , var. works, II–III a.d.—List 5

Iambl Iambl , phil., III–IV a.d.—List 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 490
